items.title: Mechanist Low Intensity Power 33k/36.5k
items.build:
1 button: https://gw2skills.net/editor/?PeSAQlxycZvMYWMOuLfxPA-DSIYRUwfGtJBqQBIz7EHJM2AvlIW2AA-e

5 buttons: https://gw2skills.net/editor/?PeSAQlxycZvsNWMOuLfxPA-DSIYRUwfGtJBqQBIz7EHJM2AvlIW2AA-e (throw mine).

5 button note: I think you can run relic of the claw instead of eagle for a very slight damage boost (it’s not much of a difference). Feel free to spam your skills off cooldown as shown.

Recommended ~50 boon duration for alacrity: https://gw2skills.net/editor/?PeSAQlxycZvMYWMOuLfxVA-DSZYBRBsFyFcooPj2kAVoAk5dijEGbg3SELbAA-e

I decided to not put the 30 boon duration variant here to not bait anytime just run my recommended set. You can also run rune of the fireworks or mess around with the build to get to whatever is easiest for you but my recommendation is 40-50 boon duration.
items.cleaned_transcript:
Hello. Please use the chapters below to navigate this guide. Thank you.

To explain the one-button build, all you want to do is spam Blunderbuss, your skill 2 on rifle, and that is it. Then you will be auto-attacking, so auto-attack and use rifle 2 off cooldown. You want to make sure you set your mech skills to autocast. To do that, hold Control, right-click, and click on the mech skill. For example, F3 right now is not on autocast, but if I Control-right-click it, F3 is now on autocast. There is a little border and a little arrow, which means the mech will basically play itself.

One thing I wanted to say about the one-button build is that technically this is not the highest you could achieve with the one-button version for Mechanist. The highest is on sword. You play the same way and spam sword 2. The problem with sword is that it is fully melee, and the DPS difference is not that high. When I was testing this, rifle was giving me about 33k DPS, and sword was giving me about 33.5k or 33.7k. The DPS difference is very minimal, so I decided I do not want to show the sword anymore. It is basically the exact same build. If you want to continue playing sword, you spam skill 2 on either weapon. Rifle gives you that range. If you are doing mechanics and auto-attacking from range, it is very easy for rifle to close the DPS gap between sword and rifle. There is really no reason to run sword. Just run rifle and make it easy on yourself. Sword technically exists, and you can run it if you wish. It is viable and strong, pretty much just as strong as rifle, and it is the same thing: spam sword 2.

To explain the five-button build, your opener is skill 3 into skill 2 into Throw Mine into skill 5 into skill 4. Afterwards, all you really want to do is press your buttons as they come off cooldown. If you want to optimize, skill 2 is your highest priority. Skill 3 and Throw Mine are similar in priority. Next would be skill 5, and then Overcharged Shot, with Overcharged Shot being the lowest priority and skill 2 being the highest priority. Technically, you also want to try to complete your auto-attack chains, meaning the two shots, so you do not accidentally cancel them. For example, if you do one shot and then cast skill 3, try not to do that. But again, it does not really matter. As long as you are casting those skills as they come off cooldown, you will probably be within about a thousand damage of this fairly easily, as long as you are using the right gear.

If you are looking to add a sixth button, your elite, Overclock Signet, would be the next best skill to press. Press that elite if you want to add an extra button, and it will add probably another bit of damage. I think the build was 36.7k, so this build could, for example, be 37.7k if you just throw in your elite. Throw that in and you will simply do more damage.

One thing I should say is that this is technically not the absolute highest five-button build that you can do. That goes to sword/pistol, but again the damage difference is so minor. I think I got about 300 to 500 more DPS doing sword/pistol, but most players, especially new players, will prefer to have range, and there will be fights where having range is preferred anyway. I think having the build be on rifle by default, even though it is a tiny bit worse, is generally going to be better for a lot of people.

The one thing I wanted to say about skill 5, Jump Shot, is that it is a pretty big animation lock, and it will also jump you to wherever your target is. Technically, you do not have to cast this. If you want to, you could replace Jump Shot with casting your elite instead for that fifth button. You do not have to cast Jump Shot, and you are not going to have a significant DPS loss. The meat of it is in Blunderbuss, Net Shot, and Throw Mine. Jump Shot will be a bit of a hit, maybe minus 1,000 or 1,500 DPS, but it will not be huge if you decide not to cast it.

The one thing I will say about Jump Shot is that you want to be a little careful with where you cast it. For example, if I am sitting here attacking the boss and I want to cast Jump Shot, but I mistarget and jump in front of the boss, you will see that I am no longer attacking the boss. This is similar to the earlier problem I think I mentioned. Basically, when your character is no longer facing the boss, your character is no longer auto-attacking, so keep an eye on that.

To explain the build, skill 2 is a big damage skill. Skill 3 is another big damage skill. Skill 4 is technically a CC, so if you are in a fight and you need to CC, use your skill 4. Skill 5 is a big movement skill. We use it for damage, but you do not have to use it for damage if you do not want to be movement locked frequently. You can just skip using skill 5. It is not a big deal if you do not cast it. Your mech skills, technically F2 and F3, are CC skills, but you are just going to be using them on autocast. Control-right-click your mech skills to make sure they are on autocast, and that way your mech will play itself and you do not have to press any of these buttons.

In terms of traits, there is nothing really that fancy, just damage, damage, damage. We take Big Boomer, which gives you some passive healing from triggering explosions, which is basically something passive that you do through auto-attacking. High Caliber is how we crit cap. If you are within 450 range of the target, you are going to be getting a 15% critical chance increase. Even though the build takes rifle, you want to try to be stacked on the group. This is the reason why you do not crit cap normally, but you actually do with High Caliber. The Mechanist grandmaster trait here is how you gain a little bit of self-quickness through your mech using its mech skills. For example, if you are playing in a group where your quickness giver may not have solid quickness uptime, this will help because you generate some self-quickness uptime.

In terms of utilities, to be honest with you, your utilities are fairly locked in. I would not change out any of them for really any reason. You can take Healing Turret if you want, but I think it is overkill healing. If you are really struggling with group healing, you can take it and maybe just spam it off cooldown to help out your healer. I would not swap out your elite. I would actually use it because this will be the best sixth button to press in the five-button build. If you do not use it, it will bring back your mech if it died, so this is your resurrect skill.

Shift Signet is a blink, so it can be useful to get across some mechanics, like Wing 1 Sabetha at the wall. Using it does stop the mech boon copy because the way the passive works is that all the boons are copied to your mech. It is a DPS loss if you use it, but it is better to use it than to die. Force Signet is technically a small CC, but I would recommend you not use it at all. Just rely on Throw Mine and Overcharged Shot to CC.

For my recommended engineer utilities, I only really have two. They are fairly basic: Personal Battering Ram and Throw Mine. You already take Throw Mine, so there is no real reason to swap. I think you should just stick to Throw Mine. If you absolutely want to CC, then you can maybe consider taking Personal Battering Ram, but again, I would just stick to Throw Mine. Engineer has a whole bunch of turrets, elixirs, and kits, so feel free to take a look through all of the utility options. Technically, Barrier Signet is useful. You can maybe consider taking Barrier Signet because it is a projectile destruction skill. For example, if you are playing Shattered Observatory CM and there are a whole bunch of projectiles, maybe you can take it. For the most part, I would not mess around with your skills. I think your skills are fine. You can maybe consider running one of these three, and that is it.

If you are interested in playing this build as alacrity, you are going to need to make a few changes. First, you are going to need to take the middle trait in Mechanist, Mech Frame: Channeling Conduits. Second, you are going to need to take the middle grandmaster trait in Mechanist, Mech Core: Barrier Engine. Swap both of your last traits in Mechanist to the middle traits. The first middle trait basically makes it so your mech will give alacrity when it applies barrier. The second trait will make it so your mech will passively give barrier. They work in tandem. Your mech will passively give barrier, and you also get a pretty nice alacrity, stability, protection, and aegis skill that your mech will autocast. This is good stuff.

In terms of boon duration, you are going to need some boon duration. The absolute minimum is 30%, but there are a few problems with the minimum for the low-intensity variant. You are probably a beginner, people are running around, people are doing mechanics, and things are not good. Basically, 30% is very aggressive in terms of boon duration. You may actually have to use your mech skills manually if they are not automatically going off fast enough to make sure that you do not drop alacrity. Overall, it is a little more spotty with 30%, but that is the absolute bare minimum that you need. I am going to leave you my recommended set. This one has about 50% boon duration, so feel free to run that. I will also probably leave a 30% variant. That is the absolute minimum, but again, I recommend you run a little bit more. You are probably a beginner, your people are running around, and maybe you are running around.

One of the annoying things about this build is that since your mech gives alacrity, you are going to have to use Return to Me! and Attack My Target! to keep an eye on it and where it is. If you mess that up and your mech uses its skills elsewhere, I think the extra boon duration will help out immensely. Again, I will leave you both variants. Run whatever you want. 30% is extremely aggressive, and you may drop alacrity or have to manually use these skills yourself. Or you can run a little bit more of a chill alacrity build that I will link to you. I will do a little showcase just to show you that you overcap on alacrity fairly convincingly.

As you can see, even in the showcase that I did, there was a brief period of time in the beginning where I did not have alacrity because my mech was not using its mech skills. One thing you could do, if you are willing, is manually cast one of these mech skills to make sure that you are pulsing out alacrity right at the start of the fight. I would seriously recommend you probably run a little bit more boon duration than 30%. 30% is the min-max absolute minimum, but you do not need to min-max to beat any content in this game, at least not any content that you will likely be playing, like normal raids or maybe some CMs. Again, I would recommend you run a little bit more boon duration. If you do it, I think you will be fine. If you decide to stick with 30%, you may have some problems, but if you do have problems, you can always put on more boon duration. That is all I had for the build. Thank you all for watching, and I hope you all enjoy the build. Thank you so much.

items.title: 2 Button Mechanist Condi 35.7k Realistic
items.cleaned_transcript:
Hey y'all. For the build rundown, more or less the opener is Spear 3 into Spear 2, and then you want to use Spear 3 and Spear 2 if they are up after an auto attack. I don't play this class very much, so I don't have a really strong read on the spear auto, so I'm sure the number in the video can probably be improved upon. The damage is already going to be good, though.

One thing you want to make sure of is that your mech skills are set on autocast. The way you do it is you hold Control and right-click all your mech skills.

For your notable spear skills, Spear 4 is a CC. Spear 5 can be used as a damage increase in the build, but it's also a mobility skill if that's something you wish to save it for.

For notable utilities, Healing Turret is an AoE heal and condition cleanse option for your team. Overclock Signet lets you respawn your mech, so you can save it to respawn your mech if it dies for whatever reason. Shift Signet you probably want to save unless it's absolutely necessary, but it is a blink. Force Signet can be used as extra CC. Superconducting Signet you also want to save. You can technically use it as a DPS increase, but you would want to switch your grandmaster trait so the signets keep providing their passive bonuses. If you want to play it the way I presented, then your autoattack is going to be your best option. If you want to swap out utility skills, you would probably swap Force Signet in this case. Barrier Signet is a projectile block. Rocket Boots is extra mobility. Personal Battering Ram is extra CC. Throw Mine is a CC skill and a boon strip skill.

Engineer also has a lot of kits, which I kind of look at as weapon-swap-ish, so I don't use them in these builds. Those are some of the more notable ones. Thank you for watching and I hope you all enjoy the build.
